name: TP-Link Omada
author: InSpectre Community
description: Integrates with a TP-Link Omada SDN Controller (v5.13+) using the Open API (OAuth2 client credentials) to discover devices and enrich inventory with client metadata.

config_schema.label: Controller Host
config_schema.help: Full URL of your Omada Controller including port, e.g. https://192.168.1.1:8043.

config_schema.label: Client ID
config_schema.help: Client ID from Settings > Platform Integration > New App in your Omada controller.

config_schema.label: Client Secret
config_schema.help: Client Secret from Settings > Platform Integration > New App in your Omada controller.

config_schema.label: Site ID
config_schema.help: Your Omada site ID. Log into the Omada web UI, navigate to your site, then look at the URL: it will be https://controller:8043/#/site/CONTROLLERID/SITEID/... — the SITEID is the second long hex string. Copy and paste it here.

config_schema.label: Verify SSL Certificate
config_schema.help: Disable if your controller uses a self-signed certificate (common for home installs).

get_controller_id.description: Fetch the Omada controller ID required as a path prefix. No auth required.

get_token.description: Exchange client credentials for a short-lived access token (valid 2 hours).

get_clients.description: Retrieve currently connected clients for the configured site (online only).

get_all_clients.description: Retrieve all clients including recently disconnected (v2 POST endpoint).

block_client.description: Block a client by MAC address.

unblock_client.description: Unblock a previously blocked client by MAC address.

test_connection.description: Verify connectivity and credentials by fetching one page of clients.
